steps.title: Capture the current baseline
actions: Record critical flows, states, viewport behavior, accessibility, performance, and browser behavior.
Create representative screenshots and reproducible fixtures.
Separate known defects from intentional constraints.
exit_criteria: Current behavior and risk are reproducible before code changes.

steps.title: Diagnose causes, not symptoms
actions: Map user and business problems to structure, content, visual, interaction, and implementation causes.
Identify what already works and must not regress.
Prioritize changes by outcome and evidence.
exit_criteria: Every proposed change addresses a named problem or measured risk.

steps.title: Compare redesign directions
actions: Decompose current and reference visual systems.
Generate direction candidates against real difficult content.
Score distinctiveness, clarity, system fit, accessibility, responsiveness, and cost.
exit_criteria: The chosen direction improves named problems without discarding useful product identity.

steps.title: Plan compatibility and adoption
actions: Map old tokens and components to new contracts.
Choose incremental route or component seams.
Define telemetry, rollback, and deprecation windows.
exit_criteria: A representative critical flow can migrate without a full-system cutover.

steps.title: Implement and compare continuously
actions: Build foundations and components from complete contracts.
Use baseline fixtures to compare behavior and visual hierarchy.
Track intentional differences separately from regressions.
exit_criteria: The new path covers existing tasks and improves the prioritized outcomes.

steps.title: Gate and observe rollout
actions: Run completion gates against old and new paths.
Compare outcome, error, accessibility, and performance signals and rollback on defined thresholds.
exit_criteria: Migration meets success thresholds and deprecation conditions are satisfied.
